title: [CI:BUILD] Packit: install golist before updating downstream spec
body:
The default Packit sandbox environment that runs Packit tasks for
downstream Fedora does not have golist installed by default and can't
run superuser tasks.
    
This commit will download and extract the golist binary from the Fedora
rpm and use it to provide golist.
    
The GOPATH mention in `rpm/update-spec-provides.sh` is only required for
golist to generate the gopaths and doesn't affect upstream or the rpm spec.
    
Currently, the only way to reliably test this is on an open github issue by running
`/packit propose-downstream`. This can't be run on an open PR.
The job-specific packit actions can only be tested via the packit
service and not via packit cli.

@mtrmac @vrothberg PTAL

comments.body:
> Or is the best way to deal with this to merge and let the next triggered job prove that the script works, because other testing is not all that convenient? That’s fine with me as well :)

There's a local test environment, but I haven't yet fully digested it. I'll work on that first to avoid future surprises. Thanks
I really know nothing about this; if you as the expert think merging & testing live is the most productive approach, I’m totally fine with it. I just wanted to understand what the plan is.
@mtrmac Thank you, I'll keep this open for now and merge it once I have a better understanding of the local testing env.

I checked with packit folks, the only way so far to test this change would be to run `slash-packit propose-downstream` on a github issue (can't be done on an open PR). And given I have greenlight from @mtrmac and that it doesn't affect upstream, I'll merge this once tests pass and try running the propose-downstream task on the github issue. I'll also unlink #2036 so it remains open after merging this PR.
